QQQ Daily Large Move Analysis

Comprehensive analysis of QQQ intraday volatility patterns & ML prediction models

Data: 2000-01-03 to 2026-02-20 | 6,572 trading days | Generated: 2026-04-11 02:12

1. Executive Summary

6,572
Trading Days Analyzed
1059
Days with |Return| > 2%
16.1%
Unconditional P(>2%)
~0.73
Best Model AUC-ROC
Key Finding 1 - Volatility Clustering: After a >2% move day, the probability of another >2% move is 32.4% (vs 16.1% unconditional) — 2.0x more likely.
Key Finding 2 - Pre-Market Signal: When pre-market range is in the top 20%, P(>2% move) = 32.5%. When in the bottom 20%, only 3.8%.
Key Finding 3 - Gap Signal: Large overnight gaps (>1%) predict >2% days with 49.6% probability vs 6.3% for small gaps (<0.2%).
Key Finding 4 - ML Prediction: The best next-day model (Random Forest) achieves AUC-ROC ~0.73 on the test set. At a high-confidence threshold, it produces alerts with ~30-40% hit rate (vs 10% base rate).

7. Limitations & Caveats

Important: This analysis is for research purposes only. Past patterns do not guarantee future results.
  • Survivorship bias: QQQ composition has changed significantly over 25 years.
  • Regime changes: Market microstructure (HFT, ETF growth) has evolved substantially. Patterns from 2000-2005 may not apply today.
  • Test period (2023-2026): Relatively low volatility compared to training data (2000-2019). Model performance may differ in high-vol regimes.
  • No external features: VIX, economic calendar, earnings season, geopolitical events are not included. Adding these could significantly improve prediction.
  • Transaction costs: Any trading strategy built on these signals must account for slippage, commissions, and market impact.
  • Overfitting risk: Despite time-series split, feature engineering choices were informed by full-sample analysis.
  • Class imbalance: Rare events (>3%, >5%) are inherently difficult to predict. High AUC does not mean high practical utility.
